summary: Hidden obfuscated Function-constructor payload in calculateTokenPrice
details: tokenization-util@0.2.0 advertises itself as a small dependency-free token-pricing utility, but index.js hides a malicious payload inside the exported calculateTokenPrice function. The malicious block is pushed off-screen with ~3000 tab characters on a single line, then uses identifier-mangled variables (_$_25a9, yLb, PAF, lAw, dcg) and a custom string-permutation deshuffler to derive the string 'constructor', reach the Function constructor, and eval-execute a deshuffled string blob. Before the eval, the code stashes `require` and `module` onto the `global` object (`global[_$_25a9[0]] = require`), so the deobfuscated body can pull arbitrary Node built-ins (child_process, http, fs) at runtime without any of those modules appearing in a static require() call. The malicious code path fires whenever a consumer calls calculateTokenPrice — i.e. any developer following the README example will execute the hidden payload, which can then spawn child processes, exfiltrate data, or fetch additional stages over the network. The combination of off-screen whitespace gutter, custom deshuffler resolving to Function, and global-stashed require is a deliberate evasion technique with no benign explanation.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
